codeReview.summary: The visual polish changes mostly match the requested styling updates, but the event detail page no longer gives concert events a full-width image/banner when artistImageUrl is absent, so the feature does not fully satisfy its expected behavior for all event detail pages.

issues.description: Concert events only render a full-width banner when artistImageUrl is truthy. Seeded concert records use empty artistImageUrl and ConcertCard intentionally moved away from photo assets, so concert detail pages fall through to the generic sports gradient branch and show no concert-specific banner/image at the top. That misses the feature requirement that the event detail page provide a full-width event banner/image for the page type.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-worker skill requires agent-browser visual verification for every feature, but this worker could not reach the protected detail pages because middleware redirects unauthenticated requests to /login and the recorded browser login attempt did not actually establish a session. The worker then substituted code review for visual verification and still reported followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for d021bdf7-7164-4286-a9f3-e207410883a0 shows agent-browser opening /products/... and /api/products/... both redirected to /login?callbackUrl=..., followed by handoff.interactiveChecks that only mention code review. middleware.ts protects all non-auth pages, and neither AGENTS.md nor .factory/library documents a reliable way to authenticate agent-browser for protected-page verification.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, relevant page/test files, middleware, and ui-worker skill. Result: fail due to the event detail implementation dropping the required top banner/image for concert events without artist artwork, plus a shared-state observation that protected-page visual verification is not documented for ui-worker flows.
